title: ClientError variants lack doc comments explaining retry/bug semantics
description: ClientError is the only error type callers ever see, but only InvalidArgument has a doc comment. Callers of a library need to know which variants are retriable, which indicate caller bugs, and which indicate server misbehavior. Without docs: Http — is it a timeout (retriable) or a TLS error (not retriable)? AuthFailed — documented as non-retriable in fetch_session/call/subscribe_events prose but not on the variant itself. Parse — caller cannot tell if this is a server bug or their own code passing bad data. Serialize — when does this occur? InvalidSession — is this retriable after re-fetching the session? SseFrameTooLarge — is the stream permanently broken? Each variant should have a doc comment stating: what triggers it, whether it is retriable, and whether it indicates a caller bug vs server behavior.
